top_signals.title: Ukraine is turning the tables
top_signals.summary: The country’s war effort was at a nadir. But mass drone production has come to the rescue
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Five Charts That Show Rising Yields Reshaping Japanese Markets
top_signals.summary: Japan’s financial markets have entered a new phase in which rising bond yields no longer signal just the healthy normalization of monetary policy, but also the risk that inflation may increase too much.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Norway Oil and Gas Producers Increase Investment Forecasts for 2026 and 2027
top_signals.summary: Norwegian oil and gas companies have raised their investment forecasts for 2026 and 2027 compared to estimates three months earlier, though overall capital spending is still on track to decline slightly from the 2025 record. The companies now expect 2026 capex to clock in at NOK 266 billion ($28.64 billion), up from the NOK 255 billion projected in February, while 2027 spending is expected to come in at NOK 207 billion, above the earlier estimate of NOK 201 billion.  Among the primary drivers of the capital spending is a NOK 20 billion redevelopment…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: America’s hegemonic glory is under threat
top_signals.summary: Along with excessive ‘guns and butter’ spending, that risk means long-term Treasuries are expensive
